title: Aranet Temperature vs CO2 Correlation as Occupancy Proxy
app: Custom HEC or scripted input from Aranet Cloud API / Aranet PRO base station
spl:
index=environment sourcetype="aranet:sensor" earliest=-7d
| eval temp=tonumber(coalesce(temp_c, ""))
| eval co2=tonumber(coalesce(co2_ppm, ""))
| where isnotnull(temp) AND isnotnull(co2)
| bucket _time span=15m
| stats avg(temp) as temp_c avg(co2) as co2_ppm by _time location
| eval co2_per_deg=if(temp_c!=0, co2_ppm/temp_c, null())
| stats avg(co2_per_deg) as co2_intensity stdev(co2_per_deg) as variability by location
| where co2_intensity>30
description: CO2 rises with occupancy while temperature may lag; a rolling CO₂-per-degree intensity metric highlights stale-air zones independent of thermostat readings alone.
value: Informs demand-controlled ventilation and space planning.
implementation: (1) Stable clock sync on sensors. (2) Minimum sample per window. (3) Review outliers after holidays.
visualization: Bar chart of co2_intensity by wing; table of high-variability rooms.
grandmaExplanation: We compare heat and carbon dioxide to guess which areas are actually crowded.
